description: Pre-seeded ARRIVAL-MNEMO memories for Phase 18 Task 1 (Security Audit). Used in Condition C only. These simulate prior audit experience that CARE-ALERT can inject when consensus drops.

memories.description: When auditing database queries, check for string concatenation/interpolation in SQL statements. The fix is ALWAYS parameterized queries with ? or %s placeholders — never f-strings or .format().

For every endpoint that accesses user data, verify that session/auth is checked AND that the authenticated user is authorized for the specific resource (not just authenticated). IDOR vulnerabilities are common when auth check exists but doesn't verify ownership.

Financial operations (transfers, purchases, balance updates) must be atomic. Look for read-then-write patterns without transaction locks — they indicate TOCTOU race conditions that enable double-spend attacks.

memories.rule: Any user-supplied content rendered as HTML without escaping is a stored XSS vulnerability. Check for f-string HTML templates, innerHTML assignments, and missing html.escape() calls.
Bare except clauses (except: without specifying exception type) catch SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt, making the program unkillable and hiding real errors. Always catch specific exception types.

memories.key_insight: Non-obvious bugs like encoding chains and numeric truncation are often missed when focus is on classic web vulnerabilities. Allocate specific attention to data transformation paths.
